description: Unexplored cut-in family: on a normal multilane road, a car in the left adjacent lane performs a slow lateral cut-in into the ego lane while closing longitudinally; a close follower behind ego makes hard braking costly, the left/source corridor is blocked by adjacent traffic, and the right escape corridor width is swept. The cut-in car is placed deterministically so its lateral path reaches the ego lane centerline at the selected TTC.
hypothesis: With a close follower and a left-side cut-in, braking should dominate at long TTC or tight right gaps, while right evasive drift/lane-change should dominate at mid/short actionable TTC with high closing speed and sufficient right escape gap.
